_meta.purpose: Canonical pillar-page registry for rrmacademy.org. Single edit point for every surface that needs to know which pillar guides exist. See pillar-create SKILL.md Gate 11.5.
consumers: scripts/build-og-index.mjs STATIC_PAGES (pillar entries merged with static non-pillar entries)
out_of_scope: static-overrides/llms.txt -- hand-curated prose paragraphs per pillar; URL list stays manual
ssot/agent-surfaces.json primary_urls -- different label conventions; manual
~/iCode/projects/rrm-router/src/index.js ASTRO_ROUTES -- separate repo, separate deploy cycle; see pillar-create SKILL.md Gate 11.5 surface 9

pillars.title: What is Restorative Reproductive Medicine?
pillars.description: The diagnostic and treatment philosophy behind RRM. How cause-based care differs from suppressive medicine, and what to expect from an RRM-trained clinician.
pillars.og_description: RRM diagnoses and treats root causes of infertility, endometriosis, PCOS, and recurrent miscarriage.
pillars.author: Dr. Naomi Whittaker

pillars.title: NaProTechnology: A Clinician's Guide
pillars.description: NaProTechnology diagnoses and treats the underlying conditions driving infertility, menstrual disorders, and early pregnancy loss. This guide covers how the method works, published outcomes data, how to find a qualified provider, and what to expect on cost and insurance.
pillars.og_description: A complete guide to NaProTechnology: how it works, what it treats, and how to find a provider.
pillars.author: Dr. Naomi Whittaker

pillars.title: What is FEMM?
pillars.description: FEMM (Fertility Education and Medical Management) teaches women to monitor hormonal biomarkers and connects them with trained providers who diagnose and treat the underlying conditions driving their symptoms.
pillars.og_description: Fertility Education and Medical Management -- a fertility awareness method and the clinical framework that grew from it.
pillars.author: Dr. Naomi Whittaker

pillars.title: What is NeoFertility?
pillars.description: NeoFertility diagnoses and treats the underlying causes of infertility and recurrent miscarriage using cycle-based evaluation, immunological testing, and targeted treatment to restore natural conception.
pillars.og_description: Dublin-based restorative reproductive medicine practice. Dr. Phil Boyle's approach to infertility and fertility care.
pillars.author: Dr. Naomi Whittaker

pillars.title: Common Questions About RRM
pillars.description: We address common questions about RRM with published evidence, acknowledge limitations, and clarify what RRM is and is not.
pillars.og_description: We address common questions about RRM with published evidence, acknowledge limitations, and clarify what RRM is and is not.
pillars.author: Dr. Naomi Whittaker
pillars.in_guides_catalogue_note: Intentionally hidden from the /guides/ catalogue UI but discoverable via search and direct link. Kept in shell nav + sitemap + navigate_to_section.

pillars.title: RRM Glossary
pillars.description: A comprehensive, evidence-based reference for the terminology, methods, conditions, and procedures encountered within the Restorative Reproductive Medicine framework.
pillars.og_description: Definitions of the terms, acronyms, and concepts used across restorative reproductive medicine.
pillars.author: Dr. Naomi Whittaker
pillars.in_shell_guides_nav_note: Glossary gets its own permanent sidebar link via the inGlossary check in AppShellChrome -- excluded from GUIDES_PATHS so aria-current on Guides does not fire on /glossary/*.

pillars.title: ART Registries and Codes of Practice
pillars.description: A global reference to national IVF registries (HFEA, RTAC, ANZARD, Q-IVF, CDC, DIR, JSOG, CARTR Plus, REDLARA, ESHRE EIM, ICMART) and the codes of practice that govern fertility clinics. What each measures, and what they miss.
pillars.og_title: ART Registries and Codes of Practice
pillars.og_description: Reference guide to national IVF registries (HFEA, CDC, ANZARD, Q-IVF, DIR, JSOG, ESHRE EIM, ICMART) and codes of practice. What each measures, what they miss.
pillars.author: RRM Academy

pillars.title: PCOS Explained: Diagnosis, Phenotypes, and the RRM Approach
pillars.description: Polycystic ovary syndrome has four diagnostic phenotypes and distinct metabolic, reproductive, and mental health implications. How RRM-trained clinicians approach diagnosis and treatment differently.
pillars.og_title: PCOS: Diagnosis, Phenotypes, and the RRM Approach
pillars.og_description: Polycystic ovary syndrome has four diagnostic phenotypes and distinct metabolic, reproductive, and mental health implications. How RRM-trained clinicians approach diagnosis and treatment.
pillars.author: Dr. Naomi Whittaker

pillars.title: Endometriosis: A Clinician-Reviewed Guide
pillars.description: Endometriosis is a chronic inflammatory disease that drives pain, organ dysfunction, and infertility. How RRM-trained clinicians diagnose and treat it through excision surgery, cycle charting, and longitudinal care.
pillars.og_description: A chronic inflammatory disease that drives pain, organ dysfunction, and infertility. How RRM clinicians diagnose and treat it through excision surgery and longitudinal care.
pillars.author: Dr. Naomi Whittaker

pillars.title: Chronic Endometritis: A Clinician-Reviewed Guide
pillars.description: Chronic endometritis is a persistent, low-grade inflammation of the uterine lining diagnosed by CD138 endometrial biopsy. It is frequently missed in recurrent miscarriage and implantation failure workups, and it is treatable with antibiotic therapy.
pillars.og_description: Persistent inflammation of the uterine lining diagnosed by CD138 biopsy. Frequently missed in recurrent miscarriage and implantation failure workups. Treatable with antibiotics.
pillars.author: Dr. Naomi Whittaker

pillars.title: Miscarriage and Recurrent Pregnancy Loss: A Clinician-Reviewed Guide
pillars.description: Miscarriage affects approximately 15% of clinically confirmed pregnancies. Recurrent pregnancy loss (RPL), defined as two or more clinical losses, affects 2 to 5% of couples. Most causes are diagnosable and treatable. This guide covers what the standard workup misses, what RRM-trained clinicians evaluate, and what outcomes look like after cause-based treatment.
pillars.og_description: Most causes of recurrent miscarriage are diagnosable and treatable. What the standard workup misses, what RRM-trained clinicians evaluate, and what outcomes look like after cause-based treatment.
pillars.author: Dr. Naomi Whittaker
